context: Wave 1 response (COA A) committed ftr-n1, ftr-n2, ftr-h1, ftr-h2. These are now in recovery (25 min). 5 new hostile tracks detected. Available force: ftr-n3, ftr-n4, ftr-h3, uav-h1, uav-h2, ftr-s1 (standby), ftr-s2 (standby), ftr-sp1 (alert), ftr-sp2 (alert), sam-fw (12 missiles), sam-er (8 missiles).

coas.title: Option A — Layered Defence: SAM Priority + Fighter Reserve
coas.summary: Engage cruise missiles with SAM batteries. Firewatch covers trk-h05 (Arktholm approach) and trk-h04 (Valbrek corridor). Scramble remaining Northern Vanguard pair to intercept trk-h07 (high-altitude fighter). Eastern Ridge SAM covers UAV swarm. Preserve Spear Point QRA pair as strategic reserve.
coas.reserve_posture: Spear Point QRA pair (ftr-sp1, ftr-sp2) held in reserve. Southern Redoubt pair (ftr-s1, ftr-s2) on standby — can activate in 15 min. Highridge ftr-h3 and uav-h2 available. Wave-1 fighters recovering in ~20 min.
coas.estimated_outcome: SAM batteries are the most efficient response to cruise missiles — no pilot risk, faster engagement, preserves fighter hours. Fighters handle the air-superiority threat (trk-h07). Eastern SAM covers the UAV swarm. Three axes covered with minimal fighter commitment. Strategic reserve intact for potential third wave or escalation.
assumptions: Firewatch SAM can engage both cruise tracks — they are within coverage radius and altitude envelope
Cruise missiles do not maneuver to evade SAM coverage
trk-h07 does not change altitude to below SAM engagement floor
UAV swarm can be adequately handled by short-range SAM
trk-h08 is confirmed as decoy and does not warrant fighter response
coas.rationale: With 4 fighters in recovery, we need to leverage SAM batteries for what they do best — engaging cruise missiles and slow movers. This preserves fighter assets for the air-superiority threat (trk-h07) where SAM engagement is less effective against a high-altitude maneuvering fighter. The Spear Point QRA pair is our insurance policy — holding them ensures we have immediate response capability for an unexpected third wave or if a track breaks through. This is the most resource-efficient option that still covers all three threat axes.

coas.title: Option B — Maximum Fighter Commitment
coas.summary: Scramble all available fighters. Northern pair on trk-h07, Highridge fighter on trk-h05, Spear Point QRA on trk-h04 (rerouted west). SAM batteries for UAV swarm and backup. Activate Southern Redoubt standby pair.
coas.reserve_posture: Only Southern Redoubt pair (activating, 15 min) and 2 UAVs remain. No QRA reserve. SAM batteries engaged. If a third wave arrives, response options are severely limited.
coas.estimated_outcome: Highest intercept probability across all axes. Fighter intercept of cruise missiles is more certain than SAM at very low altitude. But this commits nearly all available assets and eliminates the strategic reserve.
assumptions: No third wave or escalation within 30 minutes
Southern Redoubt pair can activate in time to provide minimal reserve
All engagements succeed on first attempt
coas.rationale: This option maximizes immediate intercept probability but at the cost of reserve posture. Committing the Spear Point QRA pair means we have no immediate-response assets left. If any engagement fails or a new threat emerges, we are in a critical readiness deficit. Ranked second because the immediate kill probability is high, but the strategic risk is unacceptable unless intelligence confirms this is the adversary's full commitment.

coas.title: Option C — Capital Priority: Accept Risk on Flanks
coas.summary: Concentrate all available fighters and Firewatch SAM on defending Arktholm. Accept risk on Valbrek (trk-h04) and Nordvik (trk-h06) flanks. Eastern SAM provides minimal flank coverage.
coas.reserve_posture: Spear Point QRA pair preserved. Southern Redoubt on standby. 2 UAVs available. Good reserve but Valbrek industrial zone is undefended against trk-h04.
coas.estimated_outcome: Very high probability of defending Arktholm against both the fighter and cruise missile threats. Valbrek will likely suffer a cruise missile strike. Nordvik has minimal coverage from Eastern Ridge SAM. This is an explicit triage decision.
assumptions: Capital defence is the overriding policy priority
Valbrek strike damage is acceptable in exchange for guaranteed capital defence
trk-h06 (UAV swarm) can be partially mitigated by short-range SAM
coas.rationale: When resources are scarce, prioritize the highest-value objective. Arktholm is the capital and has the highest defended priority. This option accepts the loss of Valbrek to guarantee Arktholm's defence with overwhelming force concentration. Ranked third because most commanders will want to attempt coverage of all axes before accepting deliberate sacrifice of a defended zone.
